name.en-US: On the Catholic Faith of the Greek Fathers (Contra Errores Graecorum)

description.en-US: Aquinas's defence of the Catholic faith against the Greek positions on the Trinity, the procession of the Holy Spirit, the primacy of the Pope, and other contested doctrines — written for Urban IV in connection with reunion attempts.
description.la: Defensio Thomae fidei catholicae contra positiones Graecas de Trinitate, processione Spiritus Sancti, primatu Papae, aliisque dogmatibus contestatis — Urbano IV pro tentamine reunionis composita.

sources.description: Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Part 1 — Part 1
Prologue — Part 1
Chapter 1 — How the Son is understood to be related to the Father as something caused to its cause
Chapter 2 — How the Son is to be understood as second from the Father and the Holy Spirit third
Chapter 3 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood as third light
Chapter 4 — How the essence is to understood as begotten in the Son and spirated in the Holy Spirit
Chapter 5 — How Jesus is to be understood as Son of the paternal essence
Chapter 6 — How properties of the Father are to be understood as proper to the Son
Chapter 7 — How the Father is to be understood as needing neither Son nor Holy Spirit for his perfection
Chapter 8 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood as unbegotten
Chapter 9 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood as the mean between Father and Son
Chapter 10 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood as the image of the Son
Chapter 11 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood in the Father as in his image
Chapter 12 — How the Holy Spirit is to be understood to be the word of the Son
Chapter 13 — How by the name of Christ is to be understood the Holy Spirit
Chapter 14 — How the assertion that the Holy Spirit does not send the Son is to be understood
Chapter 15 — How the assertion that the Holy Spirit truly works through the Son is to be understood
Chapter 16 — How God is to be understood as not dwelling in men before the incarnation of Christ
Chapter 17 — How the divine essence is to be understood as conceived and born
Chapter 18 — How the assertion that the deity was made is to be understood
Chapter 19 — How the Son of God is to be understood to have assumed a human nature in his essence
Chapter 20 — How the assertion that a man was assumed is to be understood
Chapter 21 — How the assertion "God made man God" is to be understood
Chapter 22 — How the likeness of the first parent is to be understood as erased in Christ
Chapter 23 — How the assertion that "the creature cannot cooperate with the Creator," is to be understood
Chapter 24 — How the assertion that "the creature does not belong to the Creator," is to be understood
Chapter 25 — How our assertion that the angels by nature are not ranked second and third is to be understood
Chapter 26 — How the assertion that even the Seraphim learn from Paul as a teacher is to be understood
Chapter 27 — How the assertion that the breath of life which God breathed into the face of man is not the rational soul, but the outpouring of the Holy Spirit is to be understood
Chapter 28 — How the impossibility of not blaspheming for one who has once blasphemed is to be understood
Chapter 29 — How the assertion that faith cannot be preached is to be understood
Chapter 30 — How the assertion that faith is not ministered to us by angels is to be understood
Chapter 31 — How the assertion that even the New Testament is a death-dealing letter is to be understood
Chapter 32 — How the sole definition of the Nicene Council is to be understood as the unique and true possession of the faithful
Part 2 — Part 2
Prologue — Part 2
Chapter 1 — That the Holy Spirit is the Spirit of the Son
Chapter 2 — That the Son sends the Holy Spirit
Chapter 3 — That the Holy Spirit receives of that which is the Son’s
Chapter 4 — That the Son works through the Holy Spirit
Chapter 5 — That the Holy Spirit is the image of the Son
Chapter 6 — That he is the character of the Son
Chapter 7 — That he is also the seal of the Son
Chapter 8 — That the Holy Spirit is from the Father through the Son
Chapter 9 — That the Holy Spirit is from the Son
Chapter 10 — That he is jointly from Father and Son
Chapter 11 — That he is from both from eternity
Chapter 12 — That the Holy Spirit is a person from persons
Chapter 13 — That he is also from the essence of Father and Son
Chapter 14 — That he also is naturally from the Son
Chapter 15 — That the Son also spirates the Holy Spirit
Chapter 16 — That the Son spirates he has from a personal property
Chapter 17 — That on the same grounds he is spirated by Father and Son
Chapter 18 — That he is spirated from the Son eternally
Chapter 19 — That the Holy Spirit is spirated from the essence of the Son
Chapter 20 — That the Holy Spirit emanates from the Son
Chapter 21 — That the Holy Spirit flows from the Son and this from eternity
Chapter 22 — That the Son also originates the Holy Spirit
Chapter 23 — That the Son is the author of the Holy Spirit
Chapter 24 — That the Son is also principle of the Holy Spirit
Chapter 25 — That the Son is also source of the Holy Spirit
Chapter 26 — The general conclusion: that the Spirit proceeds from the Son
Chapter 27 — That in the divine person to flow and to proceed is the same
Chapter 28 — That to demonstrate the procession of the Holy Spirit the Greek and Latin Doctors use the same arguments
Chapter 29 — That the Holy Spirit is distinguished from the Son in this that he is from him
Chapter 30 — That the distinction of persons should be according to some order of nature
Chapter 31 — That to believe the Holy Spirit is from the Son is necessary for salvation
Chapter 32 — That the Roman Pontiff is the first and greatest among all bishops
Chapter 33 — That the same Pontiff has universal jurisdiction over the entire Church of Christ
Chapter 34 — That the same possesses in the Church a fullness of power
Chapter 35 — That he enjoys the same power conferred on Peter by Christ
Chapter 36 — That to him belongs the right of deciding what pertains to faith
Chapter 37 — That he is the superior of the other patriarchs
Chapter 38 — That to be subject to the Roman Pontiff is necessary for salvation
Chapter 39 — Against the position of those who deny the Sacrament may be confected with unleavened bread
Chapter 40 — That there exists a purgatory wherein souls are cleansed from sins not cleansed in the present life
